ddls:
CREATE TABLE "registers" (
  "register_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each register */,
  "register_name" TEXT /* Name of the register */,
  "address" TEXT /* Address of the register */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the register's purpose */,
  "register_type" TEXT /* Type of register (e.g., temperature sensor, pressure sensor) */,
  "manufacturer_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the manufacturer of the register */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("register_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_registers_manufacturer_id FOREIGN KEY ("manufacturer_id") REFERENCES manufacturers ("manufacturer_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "bits" (
  "bit_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each bit */,
  "register_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the register the bit belongs to */,
  "bit_range" TEXT /* Range of bits within the register */,
  "variable_name" TEXT /* Name of the variable associated with the bit */,
  "mode" TEXT /* Mode of operation for the bit (read/write) */,
  "default_value" TEXT /* Default value for the bit */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the bit's function */,
  "bit_description_long" TEXT /* Long description of the bit's function */,
  "bit_formula" TEXT /* Formula used to calculate the bit's value */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("bit_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_bits_register_id FOREIGN KEY ("register_id") REFERENCES registers ("register_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "manufacturers" (
  "manufacturer_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each manufacturer */,
  "manufacturer_name" TEXT /* Name of the manufacturer */,
  "address" TEXT /* Address of the manufacturer */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the manufacturer */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("manufacturer_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "calibrations" (
  "calibration_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each calibration record */,
  "register_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the register that requires calibration */,
  "calibration_value" REAL /* Calibration value for accurate temperature readings */,
  "calibration_date" TEXT /* Date when the calibration was performed */,
  "calibration_method" TEXT /* Method used for calibration (e.g., manual, automated) */,
  "calibration_notes" TEXT /* Additional notes about the calibration process */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("calibration_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_calibrations_register_id FOREIGN KEY ("register_id") REFERENCES registers ("register_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "temperature_measurements" (
  "measurement_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each temperature measurement */,
  "register_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the register that holds the measurement */,
  "measurement_value" REAL /* Value of the temperature measurement */,
  "measurement_date" TEXT /* Date when the measurement was taken */,
  "status" TEXT /* Status of the measurement (e.g., in progress, completed) */,
  "measurement_uncertainty" REAL /* Uncertainty associated with the measurement */,
  "measurement_unit" TEXT /* Unit of the measurement (e.g., Celsius, Fahrenheit) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("measurement_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_temperature_measurements_register_id FOREIGN KEY ("register_id") REFERENCES registers ("register_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "users" (
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user */,
  "user_name" TEXT /* Name of the user */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the user */,
  "role" TEXT /* Role of the user (e.g., engineer, administrator) */,
  "user_department" TEXT /* Department the user belongs to (e.g., engineering, quality control) */,
  "user_location" TEXT /* Location of the user (e.g., city, country) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "access_logs" (
  "access_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each access event */,
  "register_id" INTEGER /* ID of the register being accessed */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user accessing the register */,
  "access_date" TEXT /* Date when the register was accessed */,
  "access_type" TEXT /* Type of access (e.g., read, write) */,
  "access_result" TEXT /* Result of the access attempt (e.g., success, failure) */,
  "access_notes" TEXT /* Additional notes about the access attempt */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("access_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_access_logs_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_access_logs_register_id FOREIGN KEY ("register_id") REFERENCES registers ("register_id")
)
